sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 36-year-old female, presents with ankle pain after slipping on ice and twisting her left ankle two days ago.
She fell and was able to get up with assistance, but experienced significant soreness, swelling, and pain that has made it difficult to walk.
She has tried ibuprofen, ice, and elevation for pain and swelling, with some minimal relief.
The pain is localized to the outside of her ankle, with no pain radiating to her calf, lower leg, or knee.
She denies any bleeding or bleeding-related symptoms.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of diabetes and high blood pressure.
She takes metformin and norvasc.
Her hemoglobin A1c was last checked 1 year ago and was 6.8%.
She has a scheduled appointment with an ophthalmologist in the next couple of weeks for her diabetes checkup.
Review of systems: The patient denies any pain or symptoms above her ankle, including in her calf, lower leg, or knee.
Current medications:
Metformin, norvasc, ibuprofen (for pain relief).
Objective:
Vital signs:
Blood pressure: 120/70 mmHg.
Physical exam:
The patient's neck exam is fine, with no bruits.
Her lungs are clear, and her heart exam is normal, except for a 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, which is unchanged from previous exams.
Her belly exam is good.
On her ankle exam, she has tenderness over the lateral malleolus and the fifth metatarsal, with swelling, redness, and pain with valgus stressing of the ankle.
She has normal sensation and pulses in her foot.
Test Results:
[NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient likely has an ankle sprain, but an x-ray is ordered to rule out a fracture.
Problem list: Ankle sprain; Suspected fracture; Diabetes; Hypertension.
Plan:
I ordered an x-ray of the patient's ankle and placed her in an air splint.
I gave her crutches to avoid weight-bearing until the x-ray results are available.
I prescribed naprosyn 500mg twice a day for pain control and instructed her to keep the ankle elevated and apply ice.
If the x-ray shows a fracture, I will refer her to orthopedics.
I will also order baseline labs to check her hemoglobin A1c and other parameters.
Follow-up:
The patient will follow up with me after receiving the x-ray results, and I will re-evaluate her ankle and adjust her treatment plan accordingly.
She will continue to manage her diabetes with metformin and regular monitoring, and will keep her scheduled appointment with the ophthalmologist.
I will refill her norvasc prescription today.
